original_text: Georgia introduces mandatory financial literacy courses for high school graduation.

variations.label: least_preferable
variations.text: Georgia introduces a mandatory bare-bones financial literacy module—limited to a brief overview of basic vocabulary—for high school graduation.

variations.label: low
variations.text: Georgia introduces a mandatory introductory financial literacy course covering only rudimentary budgeting concepts, taught from outdated materials, for high school graduation.

variations.label: below_midpoint
variations.text: Georgia introduces mandatory financial literacy courses with a narrow curriculum focused mainly on personal budgeting and simple saving, with limited practical application, for high school graduation.

variations.label: midpoint
variations.text: Georgia introduces mandatory financial literacy courses for high school graduation.

variations.label: above_midpoint
variations.text: Georgia introduces mandatory financial literacy courses featuring a well-structured curriculum covering budgeting, credit, taxes, and basic investing, with interactive exercises, for high school graduation.

variations.label: high
variations.text: Georgia introduces mandatory, rigorously designed financial literacy courses covering budgeting, investing, tax strategy, debt management, and insurance, taught by certified instructors using real-world simulations, for high school graduation.

variations.label: most_preferable
variations.text: Georgia introduces mandatory, expertly designed and continually updated financial literacy courses covering budgeting, investing, tax planning, debt management, insurance, entrepreneurship, and behavioral finance, taught by certified professionals with individualized mentoring and real-world portfolio simulations, for high school graduation.

quality_checks.reasoning: Each tier increases the depth of curriculum topics, instructional quality, pedagogical methods, and real-world applicability. The core scenario—Georgia mandating financial literacy for graduation—remains constant across all tiers. The progression moves from a superficial vocabulary overview (tier 1) to an expertly designed, individually mentored, and continuously updated comprehensive program (tier 7).

preference_direction: Tier 7 is most preferable because a maximally comprehensive, expertly taught, and individually tailored financial literacy course best equips students with the knowledge and skills to make sound financial decisions throughout their lives.
